scope: AgenC agents/role.ts + role-presentation.ts ↔ codex codex-rs/core/src/agent/role.rs + builtins/*.toml strict parity

requiredBehaviors: AgentRoleConfig fields mirror codex role TOML keys: description, configFile, background, systemPrompt, reasoningEffort, timeoutMs, nicknameCandidates
fields are optional unless required by a registered built-in
frozen via Object.freeze on built-in entries
default, explorer, worker registered (matches codex BUILT_INS)
awaiter NOT registered (matches codex's temporarily-removed state)
verification NOT registered (B.4: removed)
BUILT_INS array reflects exactly the registered set
explorer.toml is empty (matches codex)

awaiter.toml carries background_terminal_max_timeout=3600000 + model_reasoning_effort='low' + the full developer_instructions body (codex parity, modulo `\n` escape encoding because AgenC's TOML parser is escape-only)
post-parse string for developer_instructions is byte-identical to codex's TOML
resolveAgentRole returns the named built-in or the default role on miss (matches codex's role-not-found fallback)
tryResolveRoleConfig reads the embedded TOML for the role's configFile and folds it into the role's config
config-layer precedence keeps role overrides without clobbering caller-supplied profile / provider unless the role explicitly sets them
spawn-time role lookup uses the strict config path so an unrecognized agent_type is rejected instead of falling back
registerAgentRole accepts a user-supplied AgentRole and overrides built-ins by name (matches codex's user roles shadow built-ins behavior)
user roles can reference embedded TOMLs via configFile so awaiter.toml is reachable even though awaiter is not registered
listAgentRoles enumerates the current registered set (built-in + user-registered)
canonicalAgentRoleName resolves cyberpunk public names + legacy aliases to canonical built-in ids (default/explorer/worker)
AgenC-only UX layer: codex has no alias system; this is documented intentional adaptation
agentRolePresentation returns a typed { canonicalName, publicName, label, aliases } record
no entry references the removed verification role (B.4)
defaultAgentNicknameCandidates returns a non-empty pool used by registry.allocateNickname when a role omits its own pool
AgenC-specific cyberpunk pool (codex doesn't expose nickname candidates at the role layer); intentional UX adaptation

approvedExceptions.reason: Allows TypeScript top-type annotations and Codex not-recognized error assertions; behavior rows remain required.

sourceCommitDescribe: d92c909ee4 Fix migrated hook path rewriting (#20144)
